zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Abbott於1554年10月出生在英國艾可查阿培爾時，他的父親Thomas Abbott21歲，母親Anne Goodwyn20歲。他於1581年與Anne Dolan在英國艾可查阿培爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1625年5月30日在他的家鄉享年70歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭艾可可爾斯特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thomas Abbott于1554年10月出生在英国艾可查阿培爾时，他的父亲Thomas Abbott21岁，母亲Anne Goodwyn20岁。他于1581年与Anne Dolan在英国艾可查阿培爾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1625年5月30日在他的家乡享年70岁，埋葬在英国英格兰艾可可爾斯特。

nameMeanings.name: Abbott
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English abbod, abbot(t), abbat ‘abbot’ (Old English abbod) or Old French abet ‘priest’. Both the Old English and the Old French term are derived from Late Latin abbas (genitive abbatis) ‘priest’, from Greek abbas, from Aramaic aba ‘father’. The nickname was presumably a joking reference to a person's behaviour. In the US, the English name is also sometimes a translation of a cognate or equivalent European name, e.g. Italian Abate , Spanish Abad , or German Abt .

History: George Abbot from Yorkshire, England, settled in Andover, MA, in 1640; he had numerous prominent descendants. George Abbott (probably not the same man) died in Rowley, MA, in 1647. Another early migrant was James Abbott, who came from Somerset, England, to Long Island, NY, in the 17th century.

variants.name: Abate
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Antonio, Carmelo, Carmine, Gaetano, Vito, Angelo, Canio, Rocco, Sal, Aldo, Carlo.

Italian: from abate ‘priest’ (from Late Latin abbas ‘priest’, genitive abbatis). It could be a nickname, perhaps given to someone who behaved in a priest-like manner, an occupational name for the servant of a priest, or it may denote an (illegitimate) son of a priest. Compare Labate .

variants.name: Abbot
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of Abbott . The spelling with one -t is more typically Scottish than English, and in Gaelic-speaking areas of Scotland, this is sometimes an Anglicized form of McNabb .

variants.name: Abbotts
meanings: topographic or occupational name from Middle English (atte) abbotes ‘(at the, of the) abbot's (house or quarters)’, a name for someone who lived or worked at such a place, presumably a servant.
variant of Abbott , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Abbott was born in October 1554, in Chappel, Essex, England, his father, Thomas Abbott, was 21 and his mother, Anne Goodwyn, was 20. He married Anne Dolan in 1581, in Chappel, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 30 May 1625, in his hometown, at the age of 70, and was buried in Colchester, Essex, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas Abbott est né en octobre 1554, à Chappel, Essex, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Abbott, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Anne Goodwyn, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Anne Dolan en 1581, à Chappel, Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 30 mai 1625, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 70 ans, et a été enterré à Colchester, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Thomas Abbott im Oktober 1554 in Chappel, Essex, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas Abbott 21 und seine Mutter Anne Goodwyn 20. Er hat 1581 in Chappel, Essex, England Anne Dolan geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 30. Mai 1625 im Alter von 70 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Colchester, Essex, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Abbott è nato il nel ottobre 1554 nel Chappel, Essex, England, il suo padre, Thomas Abbott, aveva 21 anni e il suo madre, Anne Goodwyn, aveva 20 anni. Lui sposato Anne Dolan nel 1581, nel Chappel, Essex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 30 maggio 1625, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 70 anni, e fu sepolto a Colchester, Essex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas Abbottが1554年10月にイギリスエセックスチャップエルで生まれた時，父Thomas Abbottは21歳，母Anne Goodwynは20歳であった。1581年にイギリスエセックスチャップエルでAnne Dolanと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1625年5月30日に故郷で70歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスエセックスコルチェスターにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Abbott(이)가 1554년 10월 영국 에세스 차플에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Abbott(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Anne Goodwyn(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1581년 영국 에세스 차플에서 Anne Dolan(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1625년 5월 30일 그의 고향에서 70세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 코체스터에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Abbott nasceu em outubro de 1554, em Chappel, Essex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Abbott, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Anne Goodwyn, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Anne Dolan em 1581, em Chappel, Essex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 30 de maio de 1625, em sua cidade natal, com 70 anos, e foi sepultado em Colchester, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas Abbott родился октябрь 1554 г. в местности чаппел, эссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas Abbott было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Anne Goodwyn, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Anne Dolan в 1581 г. в местности чаппел, эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 30 мая 1625 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 70 лет и был похоронен в местности колчестир, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Thomas Abbott nació en octubre de 1554, en Chappel, Essex, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas Abbott, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Anne Goodwyn, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Anne Dolan en 1581, en Chappel, Essex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 30 de mayo de 1625, en su pueblo, a la edad de 70 años, y fue sepultado en Colchester, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
